updates.note: SPEC-23 built (collection discrimination v2 — sharper where-to-look; review §3.4/B7, addendum slice S-E): the note-08 §7 amendment (DEC-11 gate) decides the operative-pair derivation — a scenario pair is operative iff some plan×commitment verdict diverges across it in the SPEC-10 tensor, computed never curated, likelihood firewalled (K14 does not enter) — the three-way separation classification (disjoint / partial / nested; a nested expectation can never single out the inner COA, and the surface now says "cannot discriminate" instead of a second negative number; bands touching at an endpoint classify partial, mirroring O-3's edge discipline), and provenance on ExpectedAnswer (the full Provenance shape, optional slot, warning-level missing_expected_answer_provenance lint — the event matrix was the only major assessed content without a chip). The collect-next ranking now leads with the pairs the live decision turns on, witnesses rendered ("operative: R1↔R2 — P1's C1/C2 turn on it"); K11-above-K13 reproduces on the operative ranking and a pinned constructed case shows v2 ≠ v1 exactly where best-pair-anywhere misleads; the v1 numeric separations are byte-identical; degenerate states (no tensor, incomparable stamps, one live scenario, no divergence) render honest statements, never a fabricated pair. Register candidates concept §6.25/§6.26 flagged, awaiting the next batch.

SPEC-22 built (attention ordering — the scenario-weight firewall's positive half; review §3.5/M11, addendum slice S-C): the register's claim that likelihood weights "order attention and reporting, never compile" now has its visible half. The scenario strip gains an attention block rendering the K14a–c likelihood bands under the INTERVAL ORDER (research note 11-attention.md, the DEC-11 gate): R1 (45–70%) ranks strictly above the level pair {R2, R3}, whose bands visibly overlap — the system refuses, in public, to rank what the bands don't rank; no scalar sort key exists anywhere (DEC-15/19); provenance welded on, labelled "orders attention — never compiles". The S1 collection queue may tie-break EXACTLY-equal discrimination standings by the conservative pair lift of the same order — stated in the rendering, never applied across overlap, never overriding DEC-18's primary ranking — composed in the queue assembly around an untouched DiscriminationService. "Attention only" is machine-checked: a K14 band edit changes zero stamps, verdicts, memberships, or relaxation outputs while the attention block moves; import isolation between the attention/strip modules and compile/score/dominance/relax is a source-level test. Register candidate concept §6.24 (the two-behaviour sharpening) flagged, awaiting the next batch. No schema change; no oracle, verdict, or coverage-row change.

SPEC-21's DEC-30 companions shipped (comms category — projections of shipped work, not spec features; review actions A1/A2/A6): the SME-facing doctrine page ("ASSAY ↔ doctrine", site doctrine.html, linked from a Home card) carrying the review §4.1 crosswalk — which JIPOE/JPP products ASSAY computes and how faithfully, its step column now machine-audited by SPEC-21 — and the §4.4 divergence register, the five deliberate departures from written doctrine each defended up front (no weighted COA matrix, no scenario-probability arithmetic, no reaction dynamics, no civil-harm quantification, no execution model); the citation- hardening pass over research notes 02/03/05/08 naming the prior art the inventions sit in (Cooke & Halsey / Orda & Rom for time-dependent cost; Dubois & Prade, Ben-Haim, Fishburn for the four-stop verdict; Bainomugisha et al. and Hermans et al. for the glow; Fishburn and Troffaes for band-separation discrimination) plus the two date corrections (Savage 1951 in note 06; Lipkus & Hollands 1999 in note 09); and the README status fix — the public face no longer says "implementation has not started" about a repository with seven built stages.

SPEC-21 built (JIPOE anchoring — knowledge names its doctrinal origin; review §4.1/A7, addendum slice S-A): the note-01 amendment (DEC-11 gate) fixes the JipoeStep vocabulary (JP 2-01.3's four steps verbatim), the per-object assignments for all seventeen Meridian K objects (resolving K10 → step 3 — retirement does not erase origin), and the lint posture. The schema gains a JipoeStep enum and an optional jipoe_step slot on KnowledgeObject (register candidate, concept §6.23 — flagged, awaiting the next batch); fixtures are annotated and pinned by an oracle-style test; the provenance chip renders the step in words wherever provenance renders, with legends updated; a step-less knowledge write draws a warning-level missing_jipoe_step lint on the response and the delta — never a refusal, and observed is NOT exempt (origin applies to facts too). "Doctrinally shaped, not invented conveniences" is now machine-auditable, the first concrete step of the forward-derivation programme (issue #43). No banded/scalar semantics change; no oracle, verdict, or coverage-row change.

SPEC-20 built (compile-overlay precedence, fix-class — review 2026-07-14 §3.6 / slice S-B): channel materialisation now resolves overlapping region overrides by LAYER before geometry — a scenario excursion's override beats a base-knowledge override on the cells it re-describes (research note 02-compile.md §6 amendment, the DEC-11 gate; register candidate concept §6.22). This removes the one place the computed world contradicted the vignette narrative: under R3/R3m the causeway demolition now wins over K2's base engineering estimate, C5 "taken intact" scores violated, and the Stage-4 least-worst exhibit's sacrifice sets become {C4,C5}, {C3,C5}, {C2,C5} — the exhibit changed because the world got more honest, and the coverage row, walkthrough beat 4, and gallery captions moved with it in the same change (never a silent test edit). engine_version bumps to 0.2.0; old stamps stay honest about what computed them (G1); mixed-engine worlds now grey the scenario strip. BASE worlds, oracles O-1…O-4, and every other frozen-tableau verdict are unchanged.

Register batch 6 ratified (ASSAY-DEC-36): the SME checkpoint endorsed the SPEC-19 spatial/temporal UI model (findings ledger ASSAY-FIND-7), closing concept §6 item 21 — the banded-surface convention, the temporal convention, drag-to-recompute, and promotion. The surface is promoted into the live app as a fifth shared "Spatial · COA" tab over the same single store: the map refuses with the compile while K12 is contested (G5) and appears, glowing, on resolve; dragging a waypoint authors a new plan version and re-scores through the real pipeline (G1/G6). DEC-32's four role tabs stand; the spatial tab is a cross-role surface. DEC-31(d)'s never-silently-optimise caution binds.

SPEC-19 built (spatial & temporal COA visualisation, review-first): research note 10-spatial-temporal.md decides the banded-surface rule (split fill + hatch, never a single stop for an assessed channel — G2) and the temporal rule (discrete validity/ task windows, lapse marked, stated peaks quoted never curved — thesis F); a reviewable map + timeline mockup renders the real fixture geometry and compiled bands, with the C4 exposure staircase making the SPEC-18 honest gap visible as geometry (K6 propagates, K7's region is never entered, K3 is never read); dragging a waypoint authors a new plan version and re-scores through the real in-browser pipeline with value-keyed glow (G6), byte-identical to a from-scratch run (G1). Thesis states unchanged (A/B already explored; this is a presentation-lane slice). SME review invited (comms §12) before the surface promotes (concept §6 item 21(d)).

Register batch 5 ratified (ASSAY-DEC-35): civil harm stays a geometric red line, never a quantified metric (issue #41). C3 counts whether FE-FALCON fires into the populated port district — it does not weight by population. Whether firing into 55,000 is "worse" than 5,000 is a commander value judgement the system is forbidden from encoding (DEC-19). The proposed weighted_civil_harm metric is declined; the honest gap SPEC-18 found (density band compiled but not read) is a design feature.

Stage 5 & 6 blog articles shipped: "don't plan on most-likely" (SPEC-10, thesis C) and "what to verify next" (SPEC-11/12/13, theses D/E/F). Both carry illustrative widgets over Meridian fixtures — the scenario strip with interactive scenario toggles, and the sensitivity/discrimination/staleness ranking tables.

Blog backlog cleared — six owed articles from Stages 0–4 shipped: provenance chip & K10 refusal (SPEC-05), verdict playground (SPEC-07), least-worst explorer (SPEC-09), stamp determinism (SPEC-06), content addressing (SPEC-01), trace walk (SPEC-02).

Register batch 4 ratified (ASSAY-DEC-28…34): sparse channels, SPA shell, glow, in-browser pipeline, blog cadence, home-page currency, flow-view behaviours — seven candidates built under delegated authority now settled.

Stage 6 built — sensitivity, discrimination, staleness analysis (theses D, E, F live). SPEC-11/12/13 implemented over research note 08-analysis.md. K8 tops sensitivity (single-source, band-edge perturbation changes C4 verdicts); K11 ranks above K13 on discrimination (disjoint R1/R2 bands vs overlapping); K5 staleness walk flags exactly the storm-channel-dependent verdicts. 267 tests, typecheck clean.

Stage 5 built — scenario robustness (thesis C live). SPEC-10 implemented over research note 06-robustness.md. Minimax (worst-case) posture: no scenario weights, no weighted blend. The strait-early plan visibly collapses under R2 while robust alternatives hold.

Interactive system-flow infographic built (SPEC-14 flow-view sub-slice) — the S4 Bridge grown into a stakeholder explainer with a guided tour and a bounded sandbox driving the real seam (compile/score/relax), one self-contained embed in two homes. Auto-recompute is stamped and attributed, never silent; unbuilt computations (staleness fan-out, selection) are scripted and visibly labelled. Reachable from the Home page.
Communications scaffold landed — status.yml single source of truth, the blog + standalone-embed pipeline, and the first pattern-setter article (the band pill, live).
Stage 4 built — least-worst relaxation over R3m (thesis B live). SPEC-09 implemented.
Stage 3 built — the four-stop scorer and the generated handful (SPEC-07, SPEC-08).
Stage 2 built — compile to a stamped world of sparse channels (SPEC-06).
Register batch 3 ratified (ASSAY-DEC-22…27); walkthrough and findings ledger canonical.
